content:
The Centre for Disability Studies, NALSAR has opened enrollments for the NPTEL online certification course 'Technology, Disability and Technoableism'. Led by Amita Dhanda and Nilesh Singit, participants will explore how technological systems can support inclusion, but also reproduce exclusion when disabled people are not meaningfully involved in their design and governance.

Enrollment is free. Learners who wish to obtain an official NPTEL certificate may choose to register separately for the proctored certification examination. One may enroll for the course here: bitly/TechnoableismOnlineCourse

Drawing on the United Nations Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities (CRPD), the right to accessibility, the right to participation, and the principle of Nothing About Us Without Us, participants will engage with critical questions about how technologies are imagined, designed, regulated, and deployed. The programme explores not only how technology can support inclusion, but also how technological systems can reproduce exclusion when disabled people are not meaningfully involved in their design and governance.

Designed for students, educators, designers, developers, researchers, social workers, lawyers, policymakers, and others interested in building more inclusive technological futures, it offers an interdisciplinary introduction to disability, rights, and technology.

For any queries, please contact afrah.asif@nalsar.ac.in.
comment: A practical challenge is ensuring accessibility is built into procurement and product decisions early, not added later as a compliance layer. Disabled users’ involvement can change what teams consider a successful design.
